edges.description: Impaired donor utilization produces incomplete lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursors.
MPDU1-CDG can bridge CDG-I and dystroglycanopathy biology through impaired alpha-dystroglycan glycosylation.
intermediate_mechanisms: shortage of dolichol-phosphate-mannose in the endoplasmic reticulum

edges.description: Systemic glycosylation failure is associated with severe psychomotor impairment, seizures, feeding failure, apnea, and respiratory decompensation.
Multisystem glycosylation abnormalities are associated with facial dysmorphism, sensory involvement, and variable skin disease.
Severe MPDU1-CDG can include biliary-duct dilatation, renal cystic changes, thrombocytopenia, and coagulation abnormalities.
Reduced alpha-dystroglycan O-mannosylation explains the overlap with dystroglycanopathy features including muscle injury, cardiomyopathy, and congenital eye disease.
edges.target: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Severe psychomotor impairment is a core neurologic manifestation of MPDU1-CDG.
Seizures are part of the severe neurologic MPDU1-CDG presentation.
Feeding failure and poor growth occur in severe MPDU1-CDG.
Apnea is recurrent across reported patients and can occur in association with seizures.
Hypotonia is reported as part of the MPDU1-CDG dystroglycanopathy-overlap phenotype.
edges.source: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Elevated CK reflects muscle involvement within the dystroglycanopathy overlap.
edges.source: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Cardiomyopathy overlaps with the alpha-dystroglycan O-mannosylation disorder spectrum.
edges.source: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Buphthalmos is part of the severe eye involvement overlapping with dystroglycanopathy phenotypes.
edges.source: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Congenital glaucoma is part of the MPDU1-CDG eye involvement overlapping with severe dystroglycanopathy phenotypes.
edges.source: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

edges.description: Facial dysmorphism is recurrent in MPDU1-CDG.
Sensorineural hearing loss is reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.
Skin abnormalities such as ichthyosis are reported across the MPDU1-CDG spectrum, although not in every case.
Intrahepatic biliary duct dilatation is a reported severe MPDU1-CDG manifestation.
Renal cysts are reported in the severe G73E MPDU1-CDG presentation.
Congenital thrombocytopenia occurs with broader coagulation abnormalities in severe MPDU1-CDG.
Severe seizures can be accompanied by epileptic apneas in MPDU1-CDG.
Epileptic apneas can be followed by respiratory insufficiency in severe MPDU1-CDG.
The type I transferrin pattern reports impaired N-glycosylation downstream of incomplete lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursor formation.
Accumulated shortened dolichol-linked oligosaccharides report the MPDU1-dependent defect in donor use within the ER lumen.

nodes.description: Facial dysmorphism, including smooth philtrum, retrognathia, low-set/posterior-rotated ears, and hypertelorism, has been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: Slight dysmorphic features were noted including a smooth philtrum, retrognathia, low‐set, posterior‐rotated ears, and hypertelorism with megalocorneae (Figure 1B).

nodes.description: Apnea is a recurrent respiratory feature and can occur with seizures in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: All patients showed psychomotor retardation and most patients had hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, eye defects, apnea, and skin abnormalities such as ichthyosis.

nodes.description: Massive intrahepatic biliary duct dilatation has been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: Abdominal sonography as well as magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography showed a vast dilatation of the intrahepatic biliary ducts, especially within the left lobe of the liver (Figure 1C).

nodes.description: Buphthalmos with congenital glaucoma has been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The ophthalmological examination revealed a buphthalmos with slightly opaque corneae with a diameter of 11 mm and severe congenital glaucoma, requiring prompt trabeculectomy intervention.
These revealed reduced tetrasialotransferrin, with increased asialo‐ and disialotransferrin indicative of a CDG‐I (Figure 1D,E).

readouts.interpretation: The type I transferrin pattern reports impaired N-glycosylation downstream of incomplete lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursor formation.

nodes.description: Because cardiomyopathy is an important CDG phenotype and MPDU1-CDG cases include cardiomyopathy, baseline and longitudinal cardiac surveillance is relevant to MPDU1-CDG care.

evidence.snippet: Cardiac surveillance, including an echocardiogram and EKG, should be conducted at the time of diagnosis, annually throughout the first 5 years, followed by check-ups every 2-3 years if no concerns arise until adulthood.

nodes.description: MPDU1-CDG can include cardiomyopathy, with dilated cardiomyopathy and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy both reported in severe siblings.

evidence.snippet: Echocardiography detected a dilated aorta ascendens, a transient pulmonary hypertension, and a hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) at the age of 3 weeks, which developed in combination with an arterial hypertension.
At the age of 3 months, the patient evolved DCM with low output and a shortening fraction of at least 9%.

nodes.description: MPDU1-CDG can include craniofacial dysmorphism, eye defects, hearing loss, and variable skin involvement such as ichthyosis or erythroderma.

evidence.snippet: So far, seven MPDU1‐CDG patients have been described. All patients showed psychomotor retardation and most patients had hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, eye defects, apnea, and skin abnormalities such as ichthyosis.
A sensorineural hearing loss was identified with brainstem evoked response audiometry (BERA) evaluation.

nodes.description: MPDU1-CDG can overlap with dystroglycanopathies through reduced alpha-dystroglycan O-mannosylation, producing hypotonia, elevated creatine kinase, cardiomyopathy, buphthalmos, and congenital glaucoma.
nodes.id: Dystroglycanopathy-overlap muscle, eye, and cardiac involvement

evidence.snippet: Buphthalmos, glaucoma, DCM, and elevated CK are clinical features that overlap with the disease spectrum of the dystroglycanopathies.
In line with the clinical symptoms overlapping with dystroglycanopathies, we showed reduced O‐mannosylation of αDG in patient fibroblasts.

nodes.description: Elevated creatine kinase is part of the dystroglycanopathy-overlap phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Blood CK levels were elevated up to 3090 U/L without substantial elevation of aspartate amino transferases (maximal 175 U/L) and alanine amino transferases (maximal 163 U/L), biochemical signs of cholestasis or icterus.

nodes.description: Failure to thrive and feeding difficulty can occur in MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The patient has severe psychomotor retardation, seizures, failure to thrive, dry skin and scaling with erythroderma, and impaired vision.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is a frequent early neurologic and neuromuscular manifestation.

evidence.snippet: Here, we report two MPDU1-CDG patients without skin involvement, but with massive dilatation of the biliary duct system and dystroglycanopathy characteristics including hypotonia, elevated creatine kinase, dilated cardiomyopathy, buphthalmos, and congenital glaucoma.

nodes.description: Congenital glaucoma can accompany buphthalmos in MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The megalocorneae possessed a diameter of 13 mm (normal in newborn = 9.5 mm). Further ophthalmologic examination revealed a buphthalmos with congenital glaucoma.

nodes.description: Severe psychomotor and global developmental impairment is a core feature of MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The patient has severe psychomotor retardation, seizures, failure to thrive, dry skin and scaling with erythroderma, and impaired vision.

So far, seven MPDU1‐CDG patients have been described. All patients showed psychomotor retardation and most patients had hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, eye defects, apnea, and skin abnormalities such as ichthyosis.

nodes.description: Severe MPDU1-CDG can present with intrahepatic biliary duct dilatation, renal cysts, thrombocytopenia, low antithrombin III, and related coagulation abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: The MPDU1‐CDG siblings shared the following abnormalities: massive dilatation of the intrahepatic biliary duct system, small renal cysts, buphthalmos with glaucoma, DCM, thrombocytopenia, elevated CK, and low ATIII.

nodes.description: Dry, scaling skin or ichthyosis is reported in some MPDU1-CDG patients but can be absent in the severe siblings described in 2019.

evidence.snippet: All patients showed psychomotor retardation and most patients had hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, eye defects, apnea, and skin abnormalities such as ichthyosis.
The patient has a homozygous point mutation (221T-->C, L74S) in a semiconserved amino acid of MPDU1.
Whole exome sequencing revealed a homozygous missense mutation Chr17(GRCh38): g.7585994G>A; NM_004870.3(MPDU1): c.218G>A; p.(G73E) in both patients.

nodes.description: MPDU1 loss impairs use of dolichol-phosphate-mannose and dolichol-phosphate-glucose donor substrates required for ER glycosylation reactions.

evidence.snippet: CDG-If is caused by a defect in the gene MPDU1, the human homologue of hamster Lec35, and is the first disorder to affect the use, rather than the biosynthesis, of donor substrates for lipid-linked oligosaccharides.
Mannose-phosphate-dolichol utilization defect 1 (MPDU1) plays a role in the utilization of DPM.

nodes.description: Severe MPDU1-CDG causes profound neurodevelopmental impairment with seizures, failure to thrive, and seizure-associated apnea or respiratory insufficiency in the most severe infantile presentations.

evidence.snippet: The patient has severe psychomotor retardation, seizures, failure to thrive, dry skin and scaling with erythroderma, and impaired vision.
The clinical course was complicated by an increasing frequency and severity of seizures with epileptic apneas followed by respiratory insufficiency.

nodes.description: MPDU1-CDG patient fibroblasts can show reduced functional alpha-dystroglycan glycosylation, explaining overlap with dystroglycanopathy features such as elevated creatine kinase, eye disease, and cardiomyopathy.

evidence.snippet: Subsequently, we analyzed the O‐mannosylation of αDG in fibroblasts of patient P1 by IIH6 immunolabeling and a LO assay.
We found that the signals for both IIH6 and LO were reduced in patient fibroblasts as compared to the signal of control fibroblasts (Figure 2C), suggesting reduced glycosylation of αDG.

nodes.description: Small renal cysts have been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: However, at the age of 7 weeks, small renal cysts were suspected in an ultrasound scan.

nodes.description: Severe apnea and respiratory insufficiency can occur in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The clinical course was complicated by an increasing frequency and severity of seizures with epileptic apneas followed by respiratory insufficiency.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in severe MPDU1-CDG and may be associated with apnea and respiratory decompensation.

evidence.snippet: The patient has severe psychomotor retardation, seizures, failure to thrive, dry skin and scaling with erythroderma, and impaired vision.
At the age of 2 months, P1 showed tonic‐clonic seizures with multifocal sharp waves on electroencephalography (EEG).

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing loss has been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: A sensorineural hearing loss was identified with brainstem evoked response audiometry (BERA) evaluation.
Increased levels of dolichol‐linked Man5GlcNAc2 and Man9GlcNAc2 accompanied by reduced amounts of Glc3Man9GlcNAc2 were detected (Figure 2A) which indicated shortage of DPM and DPG in the ER lumen.
Retroviral-based expression of the normal Lec35 cDNA in primary fibroblasts of patients restored normal lipid-linked oligosaccharide biosynthesis.

readouts.interpretation: Accumulated shortened dolichol-linked oligosaccharides report the MPDU1-dependent defect in donor use within the ER lumen.

nodes.description: No disease-modifying therapy is established for MPDU1-CDG; reported management is supportive, including feeding, respiratory, neurologic, ophthalmologic, and cardiology care as indicated.

evidence.snippet: In her short life, the patient did not exhibit any psychomotor development, and had to be fed parenterally or by a gastric tube.

nodes.description: Congenital thrombocytopenia and coagulation abnormalities have been reported in severe MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: There was a marked congenital thrombocytopenia (minimal 45 000 platelets/μL), a slightly decreased fibrinogen (minimal 96 mg/dL), and a nonmeasurable antithrombin III (ATIII, <20%).

nodes.description: Ophthalmologic surgery may be required for severe congenital glaucoma in MPDU1-CDG.

evidence.snippet: The ophthalmological examination revealed a buphthalmos with slightly opaque corneae with a diameter of 11 mm and severe congenital glaucoma, requiring prompt trabeculectomy intervention.

nodes.description: Patient cells accumulate incomplete lipid-linked oligosaccharides for N-linked glycosylation, leading to systemic protein hypoglycosylation.

evidence.snippet: patients' fibroblasts accumulated incomplete lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursors for N-linked protein glycosylation.
These revealed reduced tetrasialotransferrin, with increased asialo‐ and disialotransferrin indicative of a CDG‐I (Figure 1D,E).
Increased levels of dolichol‐linked Man5GlcNAc2 and Man9GlcNAc2 accompanied by reduced amounts of Glc3Man9GlcNAc2 were detected (Figure 2A) which indicated shortage of DPM and DPG in the ER lumen.
